w20: Memory-optimized server featuring 80 vCPUs and 1922 GB of RAM, designed for large-scale in-memory database and enterprise analytical workloads.
c150: Google Cloud Platform m1-ultramem-80 is a memory-optimized server featuring 80 vCPUs and 1922 GB of RAM for large-scale enterprise database workloads.
c200: The Google Cloud Platform m1-ultramem-80 is a memory-optimized server with 80 vCPUs and 1922 GB of RAM. It is engineered for demanding, memory-intensive enterprise database and analytical applications.
w150: The Google Cloud Platform m1-ultramem-80 is a memory-optimized server instance designed for high-capacity data requirements. It utilizes an x86_64 architecture powered by Intel Xeon processors, providing 80 vCPUs across 40 physical cores with a clock speed of 2.2 GHz. The system includes 1922 GB of RAM, resulting in approximately 24.02 GB of memory per core. This configuration does not include local storage or GPU acceleration. Benchmark results indicate average performance across multi-core CPU tasks, database operations, and memory bandwidth metrics, while single-core performance and specific LLM inference tasks show lower relative throughput. The architecture supports AVX, AES, BMI2, and FMA instruction sets, alongside hypervisor-level virtualization. Given the substantial memory-to-core ratio and consistent multi-threaded performance, this server is suitable for large-scale in-memory databases, complex analytical processing, and enterprise-grade data science jobs requiring significant RAM overhead for active datasets.
